Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    ━━━━━━ 🌀 1. INTRODUCTION ━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Welcome, You Glorious Space Nerds

So. You've downloaded Foundation: Galactic Frontier, ignored the beginner pop-ups, joined a guild (or got kicked out of one), and now you're orbiting Discord looking for The Good Stuff™.
Congratulations—this is that stuff.

What this guide is not:
❌ A tutorial for your first hyperspace jump
❌ A bedtime story (unless you find spreadsheets soothing)
❌ A promise you won’t blow yourself up

What this guide is:
✅ For captains who want to optimize, min-max, and still laugh while doing it
✅ A breakdown of mechanics, events, upgrades, and champions that actually matter
✅ Sprinkled with: sarcasm, existential dread, and “oh hey, that’s useful” moments
➡️ Translation: it’s not sane, but it’s useful.

So buckle in, silence your in-game guild chat for five minutes, and let’s dive into the semi-sane, mostly accurate guide to Foundation: Galactic Frontier.


💡 Side Note for the Number-Hungry:
This guide keeps the math light so your brain doesn’t implode mid-scroll.
But if you want the full spreadsheets, stats, and calculations behind anything here, just ping me directly — I’ve got the numbers tucked away, safe and terrifying.
➡️ Translation: The math exists. You don’t have to see it unless you really want to.

Before diving into the real deal — a quick disclaimer:
I won’t be covering a few events that are either too simple to warrant explanation or pure cash slot machines disguised as “content.”
You’re not missing much — mostly flashing lights and regret.

📦📦📦📦 Deep Space Supply Depot 📦📦📦📦

✅ What It Is:
A “daily task” mini-event that pretends to be important.
💡 Reality Check:
You’ll earn progress naturally through normal play. Just spend your accumulated tasks before the 2-day timer ends, or they’ll auto-convert into 10 Credits each — basically space dust.
➡️ Translation: Use them or lose them. That’s literally the entire event.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
       ━━━━━━━━ 🌠 2. EVENTS ━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Where Chaos Pays Off

Welcome to the galaxy's rotating circus of lasers, loot, and last-minute coordination. Events in Foundation: Galactic Frontier aren't just shiny distractions; they’re your best shot at climbing the ladder, fattening your inventory, and proving to your guildmates that yes, you can read event descriptions.

This section includes a witty-yet-practical breakdown of the game’s major recurring events. Each comes with a brief TL;DR and a more detailed version for the strategists among us. 


🔥 🔥 🔥 🔥 Calamity Event🔥 🔥 🔥 🔥 

Quick Brief:
🔸 Duration: 2 days
🔸 Spawn rate: Every 3 hours
🔸 Max rewards (after 5 kills): 2 Legendary Crates + 5 Green Crates
➡️ Translation: It’s like an alarm clock that hates you and goes off every 3 hours until you deal with it.

Detailed:
The Calamity Event is a two-day sprint of chaos management and laser diplomacy. Every 3 hours, a calamity spawns — like clockwork, but angrier.
You and your guildmates must vaporize 5 calamities per day to grab the full haul: Legendary Crates stuffed with Cocoun Power Crystals, Blueprints, and other upgrade bits.
✅ Success = loot, upgrades, and bragging rights
❌ Failure = missed rewards and disappointed guildmates

Strategy Tips:
✅ Organize guild members into time zone–based strike teams
✅ Use Discord pings, calendar syncs, or your resident insomniac who’s somehow always online
✅ Remember: each player’s progress is individual, but cooperation makes it vastly more efficient
➡️ Translation: You can solo it, but why suffer alone when you can suffer together?

Senior Server Note:
On older servers, calamities take longer to burn down, but the rewards are still achievable.
✅ Organize to keep things efficient
❌ Don’t rely on sheer stubbornness — it’ll just waste time
➡️ Best play: coordination still pays off, even if your server has been around when Crim Hunt was relevant.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🚦🚦🚦🚦 Trade Route Defense🚦🚦🚦🚦

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Every 5 days (because stress needs a cooldown)
🔸 Objective: Defend a trade convoy through 3 escalating phases
🔸 Escort rule: Boosts damage, but misuse it and your ship will cry quietly in the hangar

➡️ Translation: Space tower defense, except you are the towers and everything wants you dead.

Detailed:
Trade Route Defense is a strategic tug-of-war disguised as TD in space. Your job: protect the stubborn little convoy from waves of enemies that seem personally offended by trade.

Each run is divided into 3 phases:

🔸 Phase 1: Swarm of small ships — hit fast, don’t escort unless you’re seriously under-manned
🔸 Phase 2: Shrine-heavy chaos — spread damage, avoid early kills on big targets, and consider 1 escort if too many mediums pile on
🔸 Phase 3: One very angry, very large ship — everyone escorts. It’s mandatory. Think of it as a company meeting with lasers

💡 Reward Note:
This event isn’t just fun — it’s profitable. It’s one of the only sources of Blueprint – Opportunity, the rare currency required to meaningfully upgrade one of your flagships.
➡️ Translation: Yes, you want these rewards. Yes, your guild wants you to show up.

Advanced Strategy:
✅ Always pick the highest level available — deaths are temporary, rewards are eternal
✅ Use the new Auto-Join system — even offline members contribute
✅ Adjust escorts:
🔸 If auto-join numbers are low → assign up to 5 escorts in Phase 1 & 2
🔸 Phase 2 swarm >6 mediums? → use one sacrificial escort, then full damage
🔸 Phase 3 → full-escort zone, no excuses

❌ Don’t retreat — ships revive, this isn’t Dark Souls
➡️ Smart guilds rotate escorts based on live feedback

Participation Notes:
✅ Rewards are tied to participation, not just raw damage
✅ Even low damage = solid rewards
✅ Escort decisions matter most when convoy is under fire

Senior Server Note:
➡️ Clearing the highest tier will not be an issue. Coordinated escort rotations aren’t necessary.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🏋️🏋️🏋️🏋️ Top 100 Galactic Traders 🏋️🏋️🏋️🏋️

📜 Overview
This 6-day event is a server-only competition — no cross-server madness here.
Each day offers 4 milestone rewards and contributes to several “Best of” leaderboards.
Gram Blueprints for building your flagship — and Computational Components for the GvG research tree.
That final milestone costs a small galaxy’s worth of resources for little return.
If you’re not overflowing with materials (you aren’t), aim for the 3rd chest and call it a day.

🏆 Leaderboards
Daily Ranking:
A few Credits and scraps of resources — nice, but not worth chasing.
Overall Ranking (Days 1–5):
Combines all points for the week.
Top spots grant a solid pile of Gram Blueprints — aim for top-tier rewards, not #1.
There’s always that one spender going supernova.
Final-Day PvP Ranking:
Scored on Glory Points.
Great reward pool (again: Gram Blueprints) and conveniently overlaps with Disciple Hunting Grounds, letting you double-dip rewards if you’re active.

📅 Daily Breakdown
🛠️ Day 1
Tip: Easy start — do your tributes, spend some speedups. Don’t overdo it.

🎖️ Day 2
Pro Tip: Commissions stay active 18 hours — save them from the previous day for a strong start.
Spend moderately on crystals; patience pays.

🧬 Day 3
Use a few Beacons and their drops for points.
Don’t spend Computational Components — save them for GvG.
Fill remaining points with Speedups.

🚀 Day 4
Stick to commissions only. Everything else is far too valuable for this event.
If you’re close to the 3rd chest, check whether a flagship upgrade gets you there — otherwise hold off.

⚙️ Day 5
Rewards drop hard here — use leftovers but don’t drain rare resources.
Hit the 3rd milestone if you can; skip the 4th without regret.

⚔️ Day 6
Do PvP following server etiquette.
Combines perfectly with Disciple Hunting Grounds — fight smart and farm both events at once.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🌟🌟🌟🌟 Battle Trial🌟🌟🌟🌟

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Weekly, lasts 4 days
🔸 Structure: 9 selectable difficulty tiers, each with 28 levels
🔸 Rewards: Persistent progress + long-term god-tier loot
➡️ Translation: A grind that resets weekly, but rewards you for sticking with it longer than your sanity lasts.

Detailed:
Battle Trial is your personal test of strength—and patience. Each tier comes with 28 fights:
🔸 3 waves of minions
🔸 1 Elite Judicator boss fight
🔸 Repeat until your fleet collapses or your coffee runs out

There are 9 difficulty tiers to choose from at the start:
✅ Progress carries over week to week
✅ You can push higher over time without losing earlier rewards
❌ Picking a tier too high will make you cry quietly into your AP potions
➡️ Translation: Start where you can actually win, climb when you’re ready.

Combat Strategy:
✅ Use ship cycling → pull out damaged ships, heal, return
✅ Coordinate guild rallies for bosses → no solo hero attempts
✅ Build tank Gram champions to absorb punishment at higher levels
❌ Don’t ignore the grind — skipping fights = skipping weeks of rewards
➡️ Max every level possible each week to grow fleet strength steadily

Final Advice:
🔸 The rewards are long-term power boosters for your entire account
🔸 With the recent addition of Levels 25–28, scaling difficulty can get brutal
🔸 Be mindful: pushing too high too soon means more frustration than progress
➡️ Translation: Pace yourself. It’s a marathon, not a hyperspace sprint.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
📈 📈 📈 📈 Paths to Dominance 📈 📈 📈 📈

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Every 2 weeks, lasts 7 days
🔸 Day 1: ~4 hours of PvP at Xarnas
🔸 Days 2–7: Buffs, decrees, politics, and paperwork
🔸 Goal: Control Xarnas to crown your guild leader as Trade Prince
🔸 Main scoring now comes from Glory earned through PvP

Detailed:
Paths to Dominance is Galactic Frontier’s political playground. Control Xarnas, and your guild leader becomes Trade Prince—giver of buffs and receiver of complaints.

The event changed heavily:
❌ Taking damage and losing ships gives very few points now
✅ Most points now come from Glory through PvP activity

How to Gain Glory:
✅ 1v1 PvP
✅ Large fleet battles
✅ Star rallies
✅ Defending the Star through garrisons

Systems Overview:
🔸 Only PvP connected to Xarnas counts
🔸 Rallies and coordinated pushes matter far more now
🔸 Garrison fleets inside the Star generate steady points
🔸 Scoring is slower, but still very achievable
🔸 Aim for 1M+ points for maximum rewards
🔸 Holding Xarnas grants +10% ATK and INT to your guild

Appointments:
✅ Only affect actions started while active
✅ Only one can run at a time
✅ Auto-Accept should always be enabled by the Trade Prince

Trade Prince Powers:
🔸 Distribute 35 epic rewards
🔸 Activate server-wide decrees
🔸 Become the center of every argument for 6 days

Strategy Tips:
✅ Coordinate rallies instead of random fighting
✅ Rotate garrison fleets for survivability
✅ Consistent participation matters more than suicide rushing
❌ Passive damage-taking is no longer a viable strategy

Event Rotation:
Paths to Dominance now alternates every 2 weeks with Dominion Warzone.

Survival Guide:
✅ Participate even if your guild does not control Xarnas
✅ Follow server PvP agreements where applicable
✅ Modern PtD is slower, but still very manageable with coordination
➡️ If you become Trade Prince: congratulations. Your reward is 400 unread Discord messages.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🏛️🏛️🏛️🏛️ Crime-Hunt🏛️🏛️🏛️🏛️

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Weekly, lasts 3 days
🔸 Loop: Defeat NPCs / finish dailies → earn tokens → spawn up to 5 Deliver Arks per day
🔸 Rewards: Tokens, Clues, and a side stash of Jodie Power Crystals
➡️ Translation: A casual side event that pretends to be important while you do your usual chores.

Detailed:
Crime-Hunt is like passive income with explosions. Earn tokens from your normal gameplay, spend them on Arks, and hope for Clues. Clues = rallies = potential Jodie Power Crystals.
💡 Patch History Note:
I once crunched the numbers and proved this event was the single best way to generate Food. Then the devs patched it. Now it’s just a side hustle for Jodie crystals.
➡️ Translation: fun, but no longer a galaxy-breaking farm.

Math Time:
🔸 Roughly 1 in 5 tokens drops a clue
🔸 5 Arks max per day → anything more is wasted effort

Strategy:
✅ Treat it as optional side content — not your main grind
✅ Always cap your 5 Arks daily
➡️ Translation: Easy rewards, modest gains. Think of it as a casual 3-day “loot lottery.”

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🪗🪗🪗🪗 Kaboom Robot 🪗🪗🪗🪗

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Weekly, lasts 2 days
🔸 Loop: 3 Champions fight up to 100 waves of increasingly annoyed robots
🔸 Currency: Intel → Intel Shop → glorious loot
🔸 Focus: Fast AoE clears and efficient shop spending

Detailed:
Kaboom Robot is where your damage comps go to prove themselves. Survive escalating waves of robots in 5-minute runs, earn Intel, and spend it on one of the best value shops in the game.

The event itself has remained mostly unchanged:
✅ Kill waves
✅ Clear quickly
✅ Earn Intel
✅ Convert robot suffering into resources

Top Picks:
✅ Zora:
🔸 Passive shots can stun-lock enemies
🔸 Active skill groups enemies tightly together
➡️ Combo bonus: creates the perfect setup for Lily’s bomb to delete entire waves

✅ Lily:
🔸 Massive AoE damage
🔸 Excels when enemies are grouped together

💡 Small Advanced Combo Hint:
Jodie works surprisingly well alongside Zora and Lily.
✅ Her weapon effects synergize extremely well with grouped enemies
✅ Adds additional damage layering during large wave clears

❌ Avoid overly slow single-target setups
➡️ The robots scale too fast for “eventually” damage.

Intel Shop Priorities:
🥇 Weapon Prisms
🥈 Deep Space Beacons
🔸 Cocoun Crystals
🔸 Venturous Memories
🔸 Manuals

VERY IMPORTANT:
✅ Weapon Prisms should generally be prioritized before Deep Space Beacons
✅ This applies to essentially all levels of players due to the long-term power scaling they provide
✅ Use remaining Intel on Beacons afterward

Tips:
✅ Optimize for wave-clear speed, not just raw damage numbers
✅ RNG on enemy spawns still matters — closer waves = faster clears
✅ Grouping effects massively increase clear efficiency
✅ Be careful when collecting Energy pickups during runs

Senior Server Note:
Even on older servers, Kaboom Robot remains one of the most valuable recurring events.
✅ Weapon progression scales forever
✅ The Intel Shop remains consistently useful
❌ Don’t underestimate small long-term upgrades here

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🥷🥷🥷🥷 Disciple Hunting Grounds🥷🥷🥷🥷

Quick Brief:
🔸 Frequency: Every 2 weeks
🔸 Duration: 1 day only
🔸 Cycle: Active PvP zones rotate every 3 hours
🔸 Goal: Collect energy orbs or earn Glory through PvP
➡️ Translation: A one-day PvP carnival with loot and bragging rights at stake.

Detailed:
Every 3 hours, two systems become PvP hot zones. You gain points by:
🔸 Grabbing energy orbs
🔸 Winning PvP fights (aka teaching others why you’re stronger)

Rewards:
✅ Top 10 → portrait border + buffs
✅ Top 50 → plenty of loot

Combat Tips:
✅ Orbs are safer than combat → grab, run, cash out
✅ Teleport if you’re outgunned → survival > ego
❌ Don’t treat it as a deathmatch → the goal is points, not your kill count
➡️ Translation: Think “tactical looting,” not “last man standing.”

Coordination Matters:
✅ Many servers use guild rotation systems to prevent full chaos
✅ Follow local customs → less drama, more loot
✅ Top 10 hunters → deny orbs, win fights, dominate the zone
➡️ Translation: Play nice unless you’re climbing for the crown.

Final Advice:
🔸 Going for Top 1 is brutal — expect to play every time window
🔸 The climb will drain your energy and sanity
🔸 Take it easy → loot flows just fine without burning yourself out
➡️ Translation: Secure rewards, keep your nerves. Top 1 is for masochists.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
⚔️⚔️⚔️⚔️ Guild vs Guild (Server vs Server)⚔️⚔️⚔️⚔️

Quick Brief:

🔸 Frequency: Every 2 weeks, lasts 6 days
🔸 Structure: Daily changing themes (Speedups, Champion XP, PvP, etc.)
🔸 Rewards: Computational Components + Pool Energy
🔸 Core Goal: Unlock Tier 7 Ship research
➡️ Translation: Six days of resource-burning chaos that fuels your path to T7 glory.

Guild vs Guild (or Server vs Server, depending on who’s asking) is the main backbone of progression in Foundation: Galactic Frontier. It’s where your guild’s hoarding skills, coordination, and tolerance for spreadsheets are all tested.
But — since this event is complex enough to deserve its own survival manual, I won’t bury you in math and details here.

💡 See the full guide here:
👉 https://discord.com/channels/1275441685031157902/1398279022206517268
➡️ Translation: This event is too big for one page. Trust me, I wrote a whole book about it.



💥 💥 💥 💥 Arms Race 💥 💥 💥 💥

Quick Brief:
🔸 Cooldown: 3 days
🔸 Type: Guild event
🔸 Prep: Tribute Vessel kills reduce enemy defenses (up to 30%)
🔸 Structure: 2 identical rounds of 5 waves
🔸 Rewards: Massive Guild Voucher income, guild-wide loot, and personal damage milestones
➡️ Translation: One of the most important events in the game disguised as “shoot the giant thing.”

Detailed:
Arms Race has become one of the single most important recurring guild events in Galactic Frontier.

Why?
Because the revamped Guild Voucher Shop is now absolutely stacked with high-value rewards.
And one of the best ways to obtain large amounts of Guild Vouchers is:
✅ Arms Race
✅ Arms Race
✅ More Arms Race

➡️ Translation: Missing this event is basically throwing future account progress into an airlock.

Preparation Phase:
Before launch, your guild should farm Tribute Vessels:
✅ Enemy waves can receive up to 30% defense reduction
✅ Small bonus rewards exist for the Top 3 Tribute contributors
➡️ Translation: Even the warm-up phase gives rewards for aggressive space vandalism.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Once the cooldown ends, Officers can launch the event:
🔸 On schedule
🔸 Or instantly when ready

Wave Structure:
🔸 5 waves per phase
🔸 2 phases total
🔸 20 difficulty levels

💡 Side Note:
These enemies have survived multiple balance patches:
too easy → absurd → reasonable again.

Rewards System:
✅ Rewards scale heavily with difficulty
✅ Damage requirements scale as well
✅ Always push the highest clearable difficulty
❌ Low difficulties are usually wasted value

Guild Rewards:
✅ Boosted by the MVP multiplier
✅ Based on highest single-player damage

Personal Rewards:
✅ Based on combined damage from both phases
✅ Highest reward tier is EXTREMELY important
✅ Small phase boxes still exist

VERY IMPORTANT GUILD STRATEGY:
✅ Make sure EVERYONE reaches the highest reward tier
✅ Don’t stack all strong players into every rally
✅ Spread high-damage players intelligently
✅ Let weaker players contribute enough damage for milestones

✅ Coordination directly improves guild progression
✅ Smart damage distribution matters almost as much as raw power

Core Strategy:
✅ Launch during peak activity
✅ Farm Tribute Vessels beforehand
✅ Use Officer rallies for the +30% damage bonus
✅ Coordinate rallies instead of random pile-ins
✅ Help weaker players hit milestones
❌ Don’t let ego damage-racing hurt guild rewards

Senior Server Note:
✅ Arms Race is now one of the highest-value guild events
✅ Guild Vouchers provide enormous long-term value
✅ The revamped shop contains premium progression items
❌ Participation is no longer optional optimization

Final Advice:
➡️ Arms Race is now a core progression event
➡️ Coordinate properly and distribute damage smartly
➡️ Make sure everybody eats
➡️ Except the robots.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🕶️ 🕶️ 🕶️ 🕶️ Operation Blackout: The Energy Gambit 🕶️ 🕶️ 🕶️ 🕶️

Duration:
🔸 Runs for 2 days, once a week

The Loop:
✅ Spend 10 Energy to kill Tithe Collectors
➡️ 15% chance to spawn a Tithe Fleet (max 15/day)

✅ Join Tithe Fleets for 30 Energy
➡️ Main rewards come from fleet participation


Why This Event Matters So Much:
Dominion Warzone massively increased available CC income.
❌ CC is no longer the main bottleneck
✅ Coins are

And you need absurd amounts of Coins for:
🔸 T7 upgrades
🔸 Prismatic Core upgrades
🔸 Research
🔸 Basically all late-game progression

Operation Blackout is now one of the BEST Coin sources in the game.
✅ Proper participation directly supports long-term T7 progression
❌ Ignoring this event will hurt your account later

VERY IMPORTANT:
Using the correct amount of Energy here is VITAL.
Not “nice.”
Not “efficient.”
VITAL.

Energy Setup:
✅ Around 1200 Energy/day is the modern sweet spot
🔸 ~600 Energy → Tithe Collector kills
🔸 ~600 Energy → Fleet joins

💡 Numbers may vary depending on server progression and kill speed.

The REAL Long-Term Strategy:
A lot of players still focus entirely on:
🔸 “Maximum Monday CC grind”

But the problem is:
❌ Huge CC stockpiles are useless without Coins to spend them

Think of it like this:

❌ Full Monday-only grind:
➡️ Tons of CC
➡️ No Coins to use it

✅ Strong Operation Blackout focus:
➡️ Slightly less Monday grinding
➡️ Massive Coin income
➡️ Sustainable long-term progression

<:Lily_Wow:1365308453895016448> Efficiency Trick:
🔸 4 fleet spawns/player → ~5 players needed for full joins
🔸 5 fleet spawns/player → ~4 players needed
✅ Organized groups massively increase rewards

Why It Matters:
✅ One of the best energy investments in the game
✅ Massive long-term Coin income
✅ Excellent support for T7 progression
✅ Coordination scales rewards enormously

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
     ━━━━━━━ 🪙 3. RESOURCES ━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The Galactic Economy Explained (Poorly But Effectively)

Welcome to the wonderful world of galactic economics. Resources are the blood, sweat, and credits of Foundation: Galactic Frontier, and knowing how to manage them will determine whether you build an empire or float aimlessly in the void.

This section breaks down each resource—what it’s for, how to get it, and why you shouldn’t waste your hard-earned credits on packages full of passive fluff. Spoiler: if it looks like a deal, it probably isn’t.


🌾🌾🌾🌾 Food 🌾🌾🌾🌾

What It Is:
Fuel for leveling champions. Always short early, pointless later.

Where It Comes From:
🔸 Passive gen
🔸 Missions & events

Tips & Tricks:
✅ Early → you’ll never have enough, it’s a grind, don't buy
❌ Late → worthless, don’t buy
➡️ Buying food = buying sand in a desert


⚖️⚖️⚖️⚖️ Metal & Water ⚖️⚖️⚖️⚖️

What They Are:
Needed for flagship buildings & infrastructure.

Where They Come From:
🔸 Passive gen
🔸 Trade routes
🔸 Missions

Tips & Tricks:
✅ Early → you’ll need lots, be patient
❌ Late → massive overproduction
➡️ Avoid packs unless they hide rare extras inside

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
       ━━━━━━ 🏗️ 4. BUILDINGS ━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
What to Upgrade, What to Ignore, What to Sacrifice to the Core

"Build smarter, not faster. Unless it's Core level 30. Then panic-build everything."

Your buildings are the foundation (pun very intended) of your progression in Foundation: Galactic Frontier. Knowing what to upgrade and when can make or break your pace through the game. This section breaks down building priorities, upgrade strategies, and how to avoid the painful bottlenecks that come with neglecting your infrastructure.

🔹 Building Priority Categories

🔥🔥🔥🔥 Critical 🔥🔥🔥🔥

Crew Cabins & Cargo Hold

Purpose:
🔸 Crew Cabins → reduce all building times (yes, all)
🔸 Cargo Hold → increases trade rewards, your quiet income stream

Strategy:
✅ Upgrade these early and often — they silently turbocharge your efficiency.
➡️ Translation: The sooner you upgrade them, the sooner everything else goes faster.


🔄🔄🔄🔄 Important 🔄🔄🔄🔄

Repair Cabin & Research Office

Purpose:
🔸 Repair Cabin → keeps your fleet alive post-battle
🔸 Research Office → shortens tech times & unlocks crucial upgrades

Strategy:
✅ Upgrade after your critical pair.
✅ A strong fleet means fewer repair bills and faster progress.
➡️ Think of these as your maintenance and innovation departments — vital, but second in line.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
📉📉📉📉 Non-Critical 📉📉📉📉

Main Cannon, Self-Defense Cannons, Engine

Purpose:
🔸 Main Cannon → offensive punch
🔸 Self-Defense Cannons → defensive buffs
🔸 Engine → travel speed

Strategy:
✅ Only upgrade when prepping for combat or traveling long distances
❌ Don’t bump them ahead of efficiency or research buildings
➡️ Cool to show off, but not worth delaying your next Core upgrade.


🤕🤕🤕🤕 Unimportant (Early Game) 🤕🤕🤕🤕

Ship Hangar

Purpose:
Increases ship construction speed.

Strategy:
✅ Delay until Core 15/20/25/30 milestones
❌ Early upgrades = negligible impact
➡️ Save resources for things that matter — this one’s pure late-game polish.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🛎️🛎️🛎️🛎️ Core Upgrades: The Holy Grail 🛎️🛎️🛎️🛎️

Purpose:
Unlocks higher-tier ships and buildings.

Strategy:
✅ Always plan around your Core level
✅ Keep at least one slot dedicated to Core progress
⚠️ Once a building becomes a Core requirement, it instantly jumps to Critical Priority — no exceptions.
➡️ Translation: No Core = no progress. Simple math.


📚📚📚📚 General Building Strategy 📚📚📚📚

✅ Start with Crew Cabins & Cargo Hold
✅ Move to Repair Cabin & Research Office
✅ Only then touch Cannons / Engines
✅ Leave Hangars for later

💡 “If you’re building randomly, you’re not building smart. Upgrade with purpose.”

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    ━━━━━━━ 🧬 5. CHAMPIONS ━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The Good, The Broken, and The Robot Who Keeps Stealing Kills

"It’s not about the size of your ship, it’s about who’s commanding it."

Champions are the heart (and sometimes the ego) of your fleet. From passive buffs to game-changing abilities, they can make or break your flagship’s effectiveness. Legendary champions provide better stats, superior synergy, and a satisfying sense of superiority—while epic champions are best used early on or as filler until greatness arrives.

This section breaks down champion stats, optimal team compositions, and how to squeeze every drop of value out of your most charismatic crew.

📈📈📈📈 Champion Stats by Rarity 📈📈📈📈

🟡 Legendary Champions
🔸 Boost ATK, DEF, and INT by roughly 35–40%
🔸 Strong passives and excellent synergy with late-game flagships
🔸 Their scaling compounds through passive multipliers — reliable long-term power

Strategy:
✅ Prioritize them as soon as you get one
✅ Build your team around them — they’re the backbone of your fleet
➡️ Translation: The golden standard for serious captains.


🟣 Epic Champions
🔸 Offer around 20–25% stat boosts
🔸 Perfect stepping stones for early or mid-game
❌ Quickly outclassed once you start collecting Legendaries

Strategy:
✅ Use if you must, replace as soon as possible
➡️ Translation: Solid copilots. Just don’t get attached.


💎 Ultra-Premium Champions — The True Endgame Grind

Lily (The Endless Grind):
⚔️ Earned through Guild vs Guild— and I mean earned. Outperform two guilds, repeatedly, for months. Now also available in the Arena shop.

Evan and Bee (The Expensive Shortcut):
💰 Directly purchasable — but at a hefty price tag.

Strategy:
✅ All are true endgame goals — invest only if you’re in for the long haul
➡️ Translation: Evan and Bee cost a mountain of money. Lily costs your soul.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Example Comparison

🔸 Zora Domini (Legendary) → ATK 32.7% / DEF 35.0% / INT 58.6%
🔸 Riian Dessos (Epic) → ATK 42.7% / DEF 20.8% / INT 19.7%
🔸 Lily / Evan (Ultra-Premium) → Beyond comparison — stronger at base level than most fully built Legendaries


🧠🧠🧠🧠 Champion Abilities Explained 🧠🧠🧠🧠
The Basics
Every champion comes with three core abilities — plus a fourth Ultimate, unlocked at max potential.
Abilities behave slightly differently in Radiant Combat (on foot) and Flagship Assignment (in space), but the fundamentals stay the same.

🌍 Radiant Combat (On Foot)
🔸 Primary Ability (Active):
Charge-based skill you trigger manually — usually some dramatic damage, healing, or crowd control.
🔸 Secondary Ability (Attack Pattern):
Defines your champion’s combat behavior — single-target, AoE, stuns, explosions, etc.
🔸 Passive Ability:
Always active — provides steady buffs like attack boosts, defense bonuses, or damage reduction.
➡️ Translation: Primary = big boom, Secondary = how they dance, Passive = why they boom better.

🚀 Flagship Assignment (In Space)
🔸 Active Ability:
Always scales with flagship stats (ATK, DEF, or INT).
Triggers automatically in combat — effects vary from missile barrages to defense boosts or healing.
🔸 Buff Ability:
Passive stat boost that’s always on, improving overall ship or fleet efficiency.
🔸 Diverse Ability:
The wildcard — unique perks like crit chance, cooldown reduction, or shield recovery.
➡️ Translation: Your champion becomes a customizable module — know their scaling stat, and they’ll shine.

🌌 Ultimate Ability (Unlocked at Max Potential)
Unlocked when a champion reaches max potential — a fourth, ultra-powerful ability that enhances combat or utility in unpredictable (and often hilarious) ways.

✅ Always impactful — massive power spike
❌ Requires heavy investment
➡️ Translation: The “Congratulations, you’re now broke but godlike” moment.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🚀🚀🚀🚀 Flagship Compatibility & Champion Roles 🚀🚀🚀🚀

The Basics
Each champion performs best when paired with a flagship that matches their stat focus — ATK, DEF, or INT.
Putting the wrong champ on the wrong ship is like giving a fish a bicycle: impressive effort, terrible results.


💜💜💜💜 Ion Flagships 💜💜💜💜
Ion = Aggression. Fast, hard-hitting ships that reward pure damage builds.

🔸 Jodie: ATK – ATK – Crit DMG
🔸 Lily: ATK – ATK – ATK
🔸 Phade: ATK – DEF – Crit DMG
🔸 Ajita: ATK – ATK – DMG reduction
🔸 Kama: INT – INT – INT
➡️ Translation: If it explodes quickly, it’s probably an Ion ship’s fault.


💠💠💠💠 Beam Flagships 💠💠💠💠
Beam = Survivability. Heavy armor, slow approach, hits like a freight train.

🔸 Evan: ATK – ATK – Damage Reduction
🔸 Aliya: INT – INT – INT
🔸 Doug: DEF – DEF – Damage Reduction
🔸 Klara: INT – INT – (Damage Boost for Normal Attacks)
🔸 Lucius: ATK – ATK – (Flanking Bonus)
➡️ Translation: Built like a tank, fires like a laser show.


🟠🟠🟠🟠 Kinetic Flagships 🟠🟠🟠🟠
Kinetic = Control. Balanced power and utility, perfect for INT-based setups and flexible support.

🔸 Cocoon: DEF – DEF – DEF
🔸 Eva: INT – INT – Damage Reduction
🔸 Zora: INT – INT – INT
🔸 Riian: ATK – ATK – (Damage Boost for Normal Attacks)
🔸 Lani: ATK – ATK – ATK
🔸Bee: INT - INT/DEF - INT - Boost of first
➡️ Translation: Precision, balance, and enough energy management to make engineers proud.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🧩🧩🧩🧩 Best-in-Slot Team Compositions 🧩🧩🧩🧩

Maximize buffs and synergy by matching champions to their optimal flagship roles.


💜 Ion Best-in-Slot 
Team: Jodie + Lily + Ajita

Why it Works:
🔸 All three champions scale with ATK and boost ATK-based stats, forming a hyper-focused damage comp.
🔸 Jodie and Lily pile on offense, while Ajita brings debuff removal and a bit of sustain.
➡️ Translation: Everything hits harder, faster, and louder.


💠 Beam Best-in-Slot 
Team: Aliya + Evan  + Doug

Why it Works:
🔸 Let’s be honest — Beam comps are a little all over the place.
🔸 They mix ATK, DEF, and INT scaling in questionable harmony.
🔸 Still, Evan and Doug soak damage, while Aliya pumps up INT weapons for some extra flash.
➡️ Translation: It’s chaos, but functional chaos.


🟠 Kinetic Best-in-Slot 
Team: Eva + Bee + Zora

Why it Works:
🔸 Eva and Zora both scale with INT and amplify INT-based rocket damage, creating excellent synergy.
🔸 Bee harmonizes with the high missile count of Zora, its expensive tho.
➡️ Translation: Brains, armor, and rockets — in that order.

➡️➡️➡️ ORDER MATTERS! Aliya, Eva and Jodie all buff their team, so you should ALWAYS put them first, followed by the other DPS hero.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
💸💸💸💸 Free-to-Play vs. Paying Players 💸💸💸💸

Your champion setup depends heavily on your credit limit, tolerance for grind, and ability to resist “limited-time” offers.


🆓🆓🆓🆓 If You’re Free-to-Play 🆓🆓🆓🆓

✅ Expect to field 1 Legendary per composition — your backbone.
✅ Fill the rest with well-synergized Epic champions that match your flagship type.
✅ Consistency and smart assignments beat scattershot upgrades every time.
➡️ Translation: You’re not poor — you’re strategically resourceful.


💳💳💳💳 If You’re Paying 💳💳💳💳

✅ You can comfortably aim for 2 Legendaries per team (except Kinetic, where all 3 are easily achievable).
✅ Prioritize high-scaling champions like Zora, Eva, and Cocoon — they’re reliable long-term investments.
✅ Training Manuals and Power Crystals accelerate your growth, but don’t overspend early.
➡️ Translation: You’ve entered the Quality-of-Life Zone. Welcome.


🐋🐋🐋🐋 Whale Territory 🐋🐋🐋🐋

✅ Continue.
➡️ Choose Lily, Evan and Bee. Always.
These three redefine “Best-in-Slot.” Even at modest upgrade levels, they rival or surpass most fully built Legendaries.
That said, exact scaling vs. fully maxed Epics is clear at full green — but you won’t care, because you’ll have both higher anyway.
💬 Side Note: If you’re here, you’ve officially stopped playing the game and started sponsoring it.


🎯🎯🎯🎯 Champion Strategy Tips 🎯🎯🎯🎯

🔸 Focus your Crystals and Manuals on your strongest Legendaries.
🔸 Synergy > raw stats — match champion scaling to flagship type.
🔸 Replace Epics as soon as you get better options — sentimentality is for poets, not captains.

Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
       ━━━━━━━ 🚀 6. THE SHIP ━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Your Floating Home and Occasional Explosive Mistake
“Because sometimes, space combat is less about strategy and more about what explodes last.”

Your flagship isn’t just your base of operations—it’s your main weapon, your bragging rights, and occasionally, the reason you rage-quit. Upgrading your ship correctly means more survivability, more firepower, and way fewer explosions (on your end, at least).

🛠️🛠️🛠️🛠️ Flagship Overview 🛠️🛠️🛠️🛠️
Your flagship isn’t just a space yacht with guns — it’s the core of your power scaling.
Each one defines your combat style, your champion choices, and sometimes even your sanity.

Their main abilities scale with specific stats, which means pairing the right champions is key.
Do it right, and you’ll melt fleets.
Do it wrong, and you’ll melt… morale.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🟠🟠🟠🟠 Gram — The Brawler 🟠🟠🟠🟠

Bonus: +50% ATK and INT vs. Ascendancy Followers & Tribute Ships
Best Use: Auto-Raid missions

Optimal Setup:
Convert to Kinetic, equip with Cocoon, Eva, and Zora for full INT-synergy.

Playstyle:

💜💜💜💜 Opportunity — The Merchant 💜💜💜💜

Best Use: Trade missions (and questionable “business decisions”)

Combat Note:

It’s a glass cannon in a paper wrapper: hit first, hope they don’t hit back.



💫💫💫💫 Demerzel — The Running Joke 💫💫💫💫

Can only be upgraded by spending absurd amounts of real money.

Optimal Setup:
If you must use it, pack defensive or damage-reduction champions,
cross your fingers, and accept the memes.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🔄🔄🔄🔄 Best Usage Strategy 🔄🔄🔄🔄

🚀 Auto-Raid
Use Gram (Kinetic) with a strong INT/DEF lineup.
It’s reliable, efficient, and lets you farm while you pretend to be productive.

💰 Trading
Deploy Opportunity and Demerzel — if you can only send two, these are your best pair.
Opportunity prints money, and Demerzel... drinks it.


🌌🌌🌌🌌 Breakthroughs & Prismatic Cores 🌌🌌🌌🌌

🔓 Breakthrough Mechanic
Once any module (weapons, shields, engines, etc.) hits its level cap, it demands a Breakthrough to move further.
These are milestone upgrades that unlock new potential — and cost roughly a small sun.

➡️ Each Breakthrough gets significantly more expensive, so plan carefully.

🔮 Prismatic Cores
Your key material for Breakthrough upgrades.
They’re rare, expensive, and mostly found in events, top-tier milestones, and limited-time shops.

➡️ Treat them like sacred relics — not like spare parts.


💡💡💡💡 Strategy 💡💡💡💡

✅ Time your Breakthroughs and Prismatic Core upgrades with Top 100 or Guild vs Guild events for maximum efficiency.
✅ Prioritize modules that define your ship’s role (Core for Gram, Canon modules for Opportunity).
✅ Avoid wasting cores on cosmetic progress — nobody needs a shiny hull if it’s broke.
✅ Event alignment = bonus points + better loot + no regret.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
         ━━━━━━━━ 🛡️ 7. GUILD ━━━━━━━━  
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
Politics, Perks & Occasionally Friendly Betrayal  


🌌 The Heart of Foundation
Being in a guild in Foundation: Galactic Frontier isn’t just recommended — it’s the core mechanic the entire game quietly revolves around.
Almost every system — events, economy, power growth, and even social sanity — is tied to guild activity.
Without one, your progress slows to a crawl, your rewards shrink, and your experience becomes a long, echoing void.
➡️ Translation: No guild = no game.

🏩 Why Join a Guild?
🔸 Event Access:
Many major events (Calamity, Trade Route Defense, Guild vs Guild, etc.) are guild-exclusive.
No guild, no entry. No rewards, no fun.
🔸 Buffs & Tech:
Guild technologies and research provide permanent stat bonuses.
They’re small individually — but add up faster than you can say “critical hit.”
🔸 Unity Contributions:
Your daily activity helps the guild grow through Unity Chests and Trade Prince bonuses.
More participation = stronger server-wide perks.
🔸 Social Infrastructure:
Need help with a rally, shrine, or that one boss that won’t die?
Your guildmates are your emergency support network.

📈 Guild Roles & Responsibilities
🏆 Leadership
Manages members, diplomacy, and the occasional political disaster.
Coordinates event participation and ensures unity during chaos.
✅ Lead Responsibly:
Respect your team — no dictatorship required.
✅ Share the Load:
Delegate to officers; don’t burn yourself out.
✅ Officer Selection:
Officer rank = earned trust, not random favoritism.

🔄 Daily Contributions
🔸 Help Requests:
Aid others in builds or research — costs you nothing, helps everyone.
🔸 Unity Points:
Earned through activity; vital for guild buffs and Trade Prince power.
🔸 Appointment Roles:
Always toggle Auto-Accept — it keeps buffs flowing without the paperwork.
➡️ Translation: Your guild thrives when you log in — and so do you.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🏠🏠🏠🏠 Guild Events 🏠🏠🏠🏠
Guild events are the true engine of Foundation’s progression.
They test your teamwork, timing, and how much caffeine your officers can legally consume in 48 hours.


⚔️ Guild vs Guild 
The flagship PvP event — massive territory battles with prestige, rewards, and bragging rights at stake.
Coordinate hits, defenses, and troop movements with military precision… or embrace the chaos and see what happens.
Either way, it’s glorious.
➡️ Translation: Six days of diplomacy, strategy, and low-grade panic.


🌌Paths to Dominance 
A weekly political showdown for control of Xarnas, the central star.
Whichever guild holds it crowns their leader as Trade Prince, granting mighty decrees and a flood of pings in chat.
The Trade Prince can issue server-wide buffs, appoint roles, and become either a benevolent monarch… or a mild autocrat.
➡️ Translation: Space politics, but shiny.


🌟🌟🌟🌟 Efficient Occupation & Territory Management 🌟🌟🌟🌟

🌠 Star Systems
Stars provide guild-wide buffs, but they don’t stack.
Holding several of the same tier? That’s just overkill with extra paperwork.
✅ Focus on quality, not quantity.
✅ Coordinate and rotate ownership between allies when possible.
➡️ Translation: A few good stars beat a dozen vanity projects.

🪐 Planetary Claims
Planets offer small passive bonuses — nice to have, not game-breaking.
Be generous: share planets with friendly guilds to maintain alliances and goodwill.
Hoarding them makes enemies faster than firing on a trade ship.
➡️ Translation: A happy neighbor is cheaper than a war.

👑 Overlords & High-Level Systems
High-level systems (2× Level 7, 4× Level 4) allow Overlord appointments, granting strong buffs to selected players.
✅ Assign these roles to highly active veterans or promising new players as motivation.
✅ Rotate them fairly — recognition fuels loyalty.
➡️ Translation: Power wisely distributed keeps the guild both sharp and sane.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🤝🤝🤝🤝 Server Etiquette & Rotation Systems 🤝🤝🤝🤝

Foundation isn’t just about building ships — it’s about not burning bridges while you do it.
Most servers survive on mutual respect and rotation agreements, and violating those can turn your guild into public enemy number one overnight.

⚙️ Rotation Systems
Most servers run agreed schedules for major events and system occupations.
Breaking the rotation = chaos, blacklists, and your guild’s name whispered like a curse in Trade Chat.
✅ Always check the rotation before acting.
✅ If unsure, ask — “better safe than targeted.”
➡️ Translation: Cooperation > retaliation. Every. Time.

🛰️ Server Coordination
Many servers maintain a shared Discord to organize cross-guild activity.
It’s where rotations are planned, system claims are settled, and peace treaties are mostly obeyed.
✅ Join it.
✅ Stay informed.
✅ Use it for diplomacy instead of declaring spontaneous wars.


🧠🧠🧠🧠 Social Dynamics 🧠🧠🧠🧠

💬 Be Active
Say hi. Help others. Share tips. Laugh at Demerzel memes.
The more you interact, the more your guild thrives.

🙏 Be Respectful
Everyone’s here to have fun.
Avoid drama, don’t escalate, and remember: losing an event isn’t losing your dignity.

⚔️ Be Coordinated
Event success is guild-based, not solo-based.
One uncoordinated hero can waste more resources than an entire raid team.

🛰️ Use Discord
A guild Discord is worth its data weight in gold:
✅ Share guides and pings
✅ Coordinate event times
✅ Prevent confusion (and panic)
➡️ Translation: Communication wins wars — and saves officers from migraines.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
    ━━━━━━━ 📅 8. DAILY TO-DO ━━━━━━━  
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
Your Routine in the Void, efficiency is everything, besides Prismatic Core upgrades.

🔀🔀🔀🔀 Absolute Must-Dos 🔀🔀🔀🔀

🧪 Complete Daily Quests
✅ Why: Easy rewards and steady progress.
💡 Tip: Always finish all 25 for the AP Potion — vital for events like Operation Blackout.
🧠 Pro Tip:
Some Top 100 and GvG days give points for completing dailies.
Quests stay active for 18 hours, so you can save and time them for those event days.
🎯 Plan ahead:
Hit your daily goal, then stop early — you might need them later for points.
➡️ Translation: Smart captains schedule their chores.

📦 Claim Free Shop Offers
✅ Why: Free stuff. Need we say more?
💡 Tip: Check the shop a few times per day; refresh cycles can surprise you.

⚔️ Do Your 3–4 Shrine Battles
✅ Why: Primary source for Component Blueprints and rare materials.
💡 Tip: Always fight at the highest level you can handle — efficiency first.
🧭 Coordinate with your guildmates to share spawns and speed up cycles.

⚙️ Keep Research & Construction Running
✅ Why: Idle timers = wasted progress.
💡 Tip: Queue upgrades or research before bed, during work, or while pretending to socialize.
➡️ Translation: Sleep is optional. Progress isn’t.

⏳ Spend Energy Efficiently
✅ Why: Energy is your lifeblood for events, minion hunts, and point farming.
💡 Tip: Never cap — spend it daily. Align usage with ongoing events for double efficiency.

🎟️ Farm Guild Vouchers
✅ Why: The Guild Shop sells Multidimensional Beacons and Speedups, both critical for long-term progression.
How to Farm Efficiently:
🔸 After your standard 3–4 shrine kills, aim for 10 extra per day → 2,500 vouchers each.
🔸 After your 3 weekly Holy Tribute Vessel kills, farm up to 10 more per day → 3,000 vouchers each.
💡 Tip: Max these daily if you can — vouchers are one of the few currencies that never lose value.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
📈📈📈📈 Expanded Tip 📈📈📈📈
💀 Tribute Vessels:
Killing 10 daily gives the best odds for Flagship Blueprints and top-tier loot.
If you can’t hit 10, aim for steady progress — consistency > burnout.
⚡ Energy Use:
After your vessels, spend leftover energy on 5 Deliver Arks (Jodie Event).
If short on time, prioritize Tribute Vessels instead.
🧃 Energy Boosters:
Save them for Top 100 or GvG Hunt Days, when Vessel kills grant points.
➡️ Translation: Don’t grind yourself into space dust — plan your energy like a pro.


🎯🎯🎯🎯 High-Value Extras 🎯🎯🎯🎯

👷 Help Guildmates
✅ Why: Small effort, steady perks, universal good karma.
💡 Tip: One click = buffs for everyone.

🛋️ Check Black Market Trader
✅ Why: Rotating discounts with massive value.
💡 Tip: Check 3–4× per day — best source of cheap Speedups & Crystals.

📊 Track Progress on Passes (Champion/Galactic)
✅ Why: Avoid missing milestone Crystals or Blueprints.
💡 Tip: Even quick daily objectives keep your rewards flowing.

⚔️⚔️⚔️⚔️ Holo Arena ⚔️⚔️⚔️⚔️

✅ What It Is:
A PvP arena where you deploy preset fleets, pick opponents, and earn ranking points.
Higher ranks = better loot, bragging rights, and sweet guild rewards.
💡 Tip:
The first 3 battles per day are free — after that, each attempt costs Credits (up to 15 total).
Win or lose, you still earn Arena currency, which can be spent in the shop for excellent items.
🎯 Efficiency Note:
Climb as high as you can early on for better daily rewards — but don’t stress the top ranks.
Once you reach the whale bracket, skill stops mattering and credit cards start fighting.
➡️ Translation: Do your 5 fights, enjoy the prizes, and leave the galactic wallet wars to others.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🤝🤝🤝🤝 Social Gameplay (Highly Recommended) 🤝🤝🤝🤝

🪐 Be Active in Your Guild
✅ Why: Foundation thrives on teamwork — solo play only gets you so far.
💡 Tip: Organize group hunts, Calamity defenses, or casual PvP scrims.
It’s efficient, rewarding, and way more fun than farming alone.

🎉 Run Events, Talk, Meme
✅ Why: A lively guild is a lasting guild. Morale = momentum.
💡 Tip: Host small contests, share memes, or hang out in voice chat.
The best guilds don’t just win — they laugh doing it.


🚮🚮🚮🚮 Optional or Situational 🚮🚮🚮🚮

🌐 PvP
✅ Why: Event-dependent (e.g., Showdown, Paths to Dominance).
💡 Tip: Skip unless needed — or if you just enjoy good old-fashioned ship-slap combat.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
     ━━━━━━ 🧠 9. TIPS & TRICKS ━━━━━━  
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
Stuff You’d Wish You Knew 20 Hours Earlier  
💬 “Wisdom comes from experience.
And experience comes from forgetting to spend your speedups.”
Even if you’ve crushed every event, there’s always room for more efficiency — and a few sanity-saving tricks.


⏱️⏱️⏱️⏱️ Speedup Smarts ⏱️⏱️⏱️⏱️

✅ Use them only during events that reward construction, research, or champion leveling.
❌ Don’t waste them on small upgrades — outside events, patience pays better.
🔸 Save research speedups longest — you’ll need them forever.
➡️ Translation: Speedups are like gold. Hoard first, flex later.


📅📅📅📅 Time Management 📅📅📅📅

✅ Always queue research or construction before bed or work.
✅ Spend energy regularly — don’t let it cap.
🔸 Stack progress: Level champions while burning energy on Tribute Vessels or Arks for double rewards.
➡️ Translation: Idle queues are the real endgame boss.
Don’t overextend in Top 100—hit milestones, then chill.
Coordinate your spend-heavy upgrades (Breakthroughs, crystals, etc.) to land during Top 100 or Guild vs Guild.

🔁🔁🔁🔁 Event Efficiency 🔁🔁🔁🔁

✅ Auto-Join in Trade Route Defense is a lifesaver — but only if you can’t be there live.
Manual participation yields far better rewards and lets your guild coordinate for faster clears.
✅ Don’t overpush Top 100 — hit milestones, then stop burning resources.
🔸 Time big upgrades (Breakthroughs, Prismatic Cores, etc.) during Top 100 or GvG for maximum point gain.
➡️ Translation: Automation is fine in emergencies — but captains who show up in person earn the real loot.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🛠️🛠️🛠️🛠️ Upgrade Strategy 🛠️🛠️🛠️🛠️

✅ Core first, always.
Keep at least one building upgrade aligned with your Core level requirements — no Core, no progress.
❌ Never buy Metal or Water.
They flood in passively faster than you can spend them.
💡 Use the Black Market Trader.
It’s the best value-per-Credit source in the game — real deals, not shop bait.
➡️ Translation: Your Core is the boss. Everything else just works for it.


🧬🧬🧬🧬 Champion Optimization 🧬🧬🧬🧬

✅ Stick with three champions of the same type (Ion / Beam / Kinetic) per flagship for max synergy.
✅ Prioritize Legendary Champions, especially those buffing INT or DEF.
❌ Don’t waste Universal Crystals on Cocoon, Doug, or Jodie unless you’re strictly free-to-play.
➡️ Translation: Synergy beats sentiment — match, don’t mix.


🤖🤖🤖🤖 Misc Wisdom 🤖🤖🤖🤖
✅ Overwhelmed by events? Focus on 1–2 core ones. Do the bare minimum elsewhere.
✅ Hoard Venturous Memories until an event milestone needs them — efficiency is patience in disguise.
✅ Disciple Hunting Grounds: Avoid unnecessary PvP. Teleport out instead of tanking a duel you didn’t sign up for.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
   ━━━━━━━ 💬 10. FINAL WORDS ━━━━━━━  
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  
Go Forth and Pew Pew Responsibly  
💬 “In space, no one can hear your theorycrafting.
But we’re listening—on Discord.”

Congratulations, Captain — you made it to the end of Snakebite’s semi-sane Guide to Foundation: Galactic Frontier.

You’ve learned how to optimize champions, tune your ship, exploit every event loophole known to humankind, and (hopefully) not disband your guild by accident.
Whether you’re free-to-play, pay-to-slay, or somewhere in between — your galactic journey is now better informed and slightly more sarcastic.


🎯🎯🎯🎯 Final Takeaways 🎯🎯🎯🎯

✅ Play your way. There’s no single true meta — just a spectrum of creative chaos.
✅ Stay social. Guilds and server cooperation are the backbone of this universe.
✅ Have fun. Rankings fade, but people remember the ones who made the grind enjoyable.


💬💬💬💬 Final Note from a Veteran Leader 💬💬💬💬

After years in a veteran server — leading guilds through merges, burnout, and late-night rally pings — here’s the truth:
Activity fades. Leadership doesn’t.
Be the reason players log in, laugh, and keep caring. It’s not about control — it’s about consistency.
🔸 Keep your players informed.
Remind them of rotations, events, and rules. Chaos starts where communication stops.
🔸 Balance freedom and structure.
When numbers drop, don’t abandon coordination — it’s what keeps morale alive.
🔸 Prioritize the team over individuals.
Progress in Foundation is collective. The team’s success outweighs one player’s ego.
🔸 Coordinate fairly.
Not everyone can make every event. Schedule smart, offer alternatives, keep people engaged.
Small gestures keep fleets — and friendships — alive.

💬 “Guild leadership isn’t about control — it’s about consistency.
When players see effort, they respond in kind.
That’s what keeps a dying server alive — and a great one thriving.”

Author: ak77_gaming
Content:
<@355791061303427072> 
Speaking as a F2P player at Core 18. Given that we received two free copies of Demerzel, i.e. 200 Blueprints, would you still suggest the same approach? As you laid out, I have kept gram as a kinetic ship & opportunity as a ion ship. I made Demerzel a beam ship. Would love to hear your thoughts.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Free to play doenst matter really. Whats important is this: If your guild is running only gram on beam, you should do so as well for the raids, as the bonuses carry over, meaning all Beam give 30% points bonus.
At your stage, you can really go for whatever floats your boat, if you want to be optimal:
Coordinate with your guild for a united setup for Gram. If not possible, just go for the best setup for Gram, if yo have for example more legendary parts of Beam, do go beam on it.
The main takeaway here is: If your F2P, you will only have access to one legenday hero per ship, making Kinetik and Beam equal on Gram (almost), but Ion on Opportunity always. Also, the way you can upgrade your ships via blueprints is only possible for Gram and Opportunity F2P (Demerzel takes YEARS), therefor Demerzel will be the weakest ship {there is a slight possibility for you to pull every event of Demerzel a 1,3% chance, in that case it will be better :3}
Ergo: Gram on Beam or Kinetik, whatever is strongest for you, and coordinate with your guilde to have united bet setup.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
══════════════════════════════════════════════
                             CHAPTER XI — COMBAT
        “Where Logic Goes to Die, but We Bring It Back Anyway (Sort Of)”
══════════════════════════════════════════════
INTRODUCTION – PLEASE DO NOT PANIC
🔸 Combat in Foundation works a lot like a malfunctioning quantum toaster: If you try to understand it, it sets itself on fire.

🔸 The UI lies, tooltips contradict physics, and counterattacks behave like vindictive space pigeons.

So we did the unthinkable:
➡️ We tested everything ourselves.

What follows is the first empirically correct explanation of combat
in Foundation: Galactic Frontier.

✅ Grab a towel. You’ll need it.

❤️ Special thanks to Magnox and Akinaelle.

➡️ This part of the guide is sort of an overview. 
Many of the numbers we came up with do not add up all the way, most of the time they are approximations. 
But they are close enough to have meaning.
All claims here are backed up by extensive testing and a lot... A LOT of numbers.

───────────────────────────────────────────────
                SECTION 1 — THE COMBAT TIMELINE
───────────────────────────────────────────────
This describes maxed ships, as used in all serious PvP.
➡️ (Non-maxed ships activate whenever they feel like it. We don’t talk about them.)

Every second:
🔸 You fire 1 normal attack
🔸 The enemy fires 1 normal attack
🔸 Because YOU hit THEM → YOU take counterattack damage
🔸 Because THEY hit YOU → THEY take counterattack damage
➡️ Yes. Shooting people hurts you. That’s how the universe works now.

───────────────────────────────────────────────
CHAMPION ABILITIES (MAXED SHIPS)
───────────────────────────────────────────────

🔸 First cycle activates at second 4
🔸 Next cycles activate every 9 seconds
🔸 Always in the same champion order
🔸 No improvisation, no creativity, no mercy

➡️ This creates the well-known Galactic Pain Carousel™:
4 hit events per second with periodic ability explosions.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
───────────────────────────────────────────────
IMPORTANT TIMING CLARIFICATION — VISUALS LIE
───────────────────────────────────────────────

Champion abilities are **applied instantly** when triggered.

This means:
🔸 Rockets fire
🔸 Rockets visually fly
🔸 Rockets visually hit one after another

➡️ But the **damage is applied immediately**, not over time.

HOWEVER — and this is the important part:

➡️ **Damage is still CALCULATED as if those hits occurred over time.**

What that means in practice:
🔸 Buffs are evaluated by their *active duration*
🔸 NOT by when the damage is applied
🔸 Visual impact timing ≠ damage timing

Examples:
🔹 A Beam buff lasting 5 seconds only boosts attacks that occur in that window
🔹 Rocket abilities are checked as if each rocket had its own timing
🔹 Buffs like **Killer Bee’s 3s rocket buff** DO apply correctly

➡️ But only to the attacks that mathematically fall inside the buff window.

Result:
🔸 Damage is applied instantly
🔸 Buff coverage is partial

➡️ This is why some abilities feel “under-buffed”
➡️ And why short-duration buffs don’t scale as hard as expected

This is not a bug.
This is a timing model.
And yes — it’s unintuitive.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
           SECTION 2 — THE THREE STATS AND THEIR QUESTIONABLE PURPOSES
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
There are three main stats:
🔸 ATK – makes the ship angry
🔸 INT – makes the ship explosive
🔸 DEF – makes the ship delay inevitable death
➡️ Let’s break them down.


------ATK — “PEW PEW PER SECOND”-----

✅ ATK controls:
🔸 Normal attacks
🔸 Counterattacks
🔸 Any champion ability explicitly listed as ATK-scaling
🔸 Any flagship ability explicitly listed as ATK-scaling

❌ ATK does not control:
🔸 INT-based abilities
🔸 shields
🔸 healing
🔸 anything the UI lied about

➡️ Note:
Some “ATK-based” abilities secretly scale with INT as well.
Yes, seriously.
No, we don’t know why. Sadly inconsistent.


-----INT — “KABOOM ENERGY”-----

✅ INT controls:
🔸 Ability damage for champions that list INT
🔸 Ability damage for ships that list INT
🔸 And (for absolutely no documented reason) some abilities that are supposed to scale with ATK

❌ INT does not control:
🔸 normal hits
🔸 counterattacks

INT is the stat responsible for abilities.


-----DEF — “I WOULD LIKE TO NOT EXPLODE”-----

➡️ DEF does not affect any of your outgoing damage.

✅ DEF does:
🔸 reduce incoming damage
🔸 scale defensive abilities if your champion explicitly uses DEF
🔸 increase your survival time (arguably the point)

➡️ And now the important footnote:
Cocoon’s ability text (“1500% DEF Shield!! 240% DEF Heal!!”)
is complete fiction.
Real PvP data shows 110–150% DEF total per cast.
The tooltip is wrong.
The shield numbers are wrong.
Reality is right.
And occasionally rude.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
               SECTION 3 — COUNTERATTACKS (FINALLY SOLVED)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

➡️ Counterattacks trigger when a normal hit lands.

✅ They scale with:
🔸 ATK
🔸 Damage Bonus
🔸 Command Points
🔸 Enemy Damage Reduction
🔸 Your normal-hit buffs

❌ They do NOT scale with:
🔸 DEF
🔸 INT
🔸 shields
🔸 any ability scaling of any kind

➡️ Counterattacks are, mechanically speaking:
Normal hits wearing a fake mustache claiming to be something different.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
                   SECTION 4 — DAMAGE REDUCTION (DR)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

✅ DR applies to all incoming damage:
🔸 normal hits
🔸 counterattacks
🔸 ability nukes
🔸 everything

➡️ A ship with 50% DR takes half damage.
➡️ A ship with 0% DR becomes scenery.

DR is the single most powerful defensive stat in the game.
Respect it.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
──────────────────────────────────────────────
                   SECTION 5 — COMMAND POINTS (CP)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
✅ CP provides:
🔸 bonus damage for whoever has more
🔸 bonus damage reduction for whoever has less

➡️ Observed in real PvP:
🔸 5 CP → ~4% swing
🔸 9 CP → ~7% swing
🔸 20 CP → ~15% swing

➡️ If you ever choose between:
“some minor counterattack bonus”
and more CP

Choose CP.
Always CP.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      SECTION 6 — THE ACTUAL POWER RANKINGS (AFTER REAL COMBAT TESTING)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
🔥 OFFENSIVE POWER (strongest → weakest)
🔸 INT
🔸 Damage Bonus
🔸 Enemy Damage Reduction
🔸 Command Points
🔸 ATK
🔸 Crew bonuses
🔸 Ship passives & misc. crumbs

🛡 DEFENSIVE POWER (strongest → weakest)
🔸 Damage Reduction (DR)
🔸 Command Points
🔸 HP
🔸 DEF
🔸 Healing
🔸 Shields (champ-dependent)

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
─────────────────────────────────────
                         SECTION 7 — TL;DR
─────────────────────────────────────
➡️ INT = BOOM
➡️ ATK = PEW
➡️ DEF = “Ow, less please.”

🔸 Counterattacks are ATK-based. Always.
🔸 Some ATK-based abilities secretly scale with INT.
🔸 Cocoon’s tooltip is a hallucination.
🔸 Command Points are MUCH stronger than expected.

DR > HP > DEF for survival
INT > DmgBonus > DR > ATK for winning

➡️ Never trust the UI. Sadly main takeaway. Its all fancy and big but not really.

➡️ For your personal Progression, you should look at Section 6 and go for upgrades that enhance those numbers before upgrading the others.

Author: zinkus_sprocksplicer
Content:
One thing which took me some time to learn is repair modules. Ships stationed at the home port self-repair. Ships in the hangar or on trade routes do not self-repair.

So don't use repair modules to repair minor PVE damage. After massive damage, put your damaged ships in your fleets, and station them. It is fine to put ion ships on the beam flagship for sel-repair while autorallying that fleet.

Author: havoc0918
Content: Finally, someone has been able to present me with a comprehensive guide that explains a game whose genre I acquired quite a disdain for after playing many hours and ending at nearly the same place. That place being at a reasonably high level of power, not a whale, but with aspirations and cruel intent. I quit, every time, with mostly the same amount of power, research done and money spent. The main reason was, you might ask? I didn’t have a clue of how the pieces I was throwing together actually, if done correctly, would give me a powerful being, army, or latent talent that could even give the whales fits. Now I find this manual, for this game, that does what no other guide I slogged through could do. It explained the game to me as if I were five. Now, as I write, at 70 years old this is perfect. I’ve been grinding since “PONG” hit the market in ‘77 I think. Not sure of the date, nailed the OS. Now, here I sit, after playing NES, Intellivision, 3DO, PS, Xbox, Dreamcast, PC…all them BS letters; satisfied. I’ve gotten a handle on a mass player…I can’t recall the acronym, but you know it, thousands playing the same game on a server, like Foundation. I didn’t mean for this to be War and Peace, but I wanted to express to you Snakebite…great handle btw…how happy you’ve made lil’ old me by taking a very complex game and turning it into child’s play. I’ve never written to anyone showering praise over what may seem to be mundane, but that’s part of this generation’s dilemma. Most don’t even realize that all our ocean’s started out with one drop of rain. If you understand that, the rest is easy. Thank you for reading and thanks for explaining these confounding games.

Author: ffsmaya
Content:
Hello <@355791061303427072> and thank you for an excellent guide. It was my bread and butter “growing up” in FgF.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Glad I was able to help you :)

Hello :D

So as for Rallies, its very complicated, or quite easy depending on how you look at it.

From my understanding, the values are added together when it comes to atk, def and int, however only the main lads bonuses for Dmg reduction and Dmg increase aswell as command points play a role for the raids end value. Also, his or hers setup for champs is also reflected here. Other ships may die in the raid quicker tho, as I think the dmg is distributed to all participants equally. 

Thats why, if you are strong and join a very small ship in a tough raid, you will take alot more dmg (small command points and dmg reduction of main ship). Its sort of weirdly balanced tho, since you die absurdly fast like that...

When it comes to the garrison, im pretty sure thats just a raid in disguise, with the same restrictions and bonuses.

Every other data is too inconsistent, so if you want specific data I cannot help. All in all its a bit odd, but chances are best if you let the biggest form the raid and fill it up with the smaller ones.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Hi, so Crit Rate determines how likely you are to critical hit anything. In my experience that is a bit wonky, i dont think its the actual number, there must be some modifiers behind it. Critical Dmg is the multiplier apllied to it. They effect all combats a little, since the NCP dont have crit resistance, you will have much more of an impact in that scenario.

They are factored in as final multipliers after calculation.

You cant really boost these stats individually per se, they are added with better equipment for your ships.

Content: it's a pvp game. there are ways to mitigate this. if your transport fleets are getting hit, then remove the fleet ships from them and then set them off to go transport. if you're going to be AFK then remove fleets from your flagships or garrison them on a planet. removing fleets saves your ships. attackers get a tiny bit of glory from destroying your flagships.  plus if you lose 1 or 2 fleets, then no big deal. make new ones and repair the damaged ones.  at least in this game you don't lose a lot of resources if you die.

Author: ztk13
Content: <@355791061303427072> Really nice guide, I've been following this for most things. One thing that I am still unclear about is which components to prioritise for each flagship, and what's the ideal progression prioritization for flagships, their components and hero synergy with flagship stats (I know you've covered some of this in different sections, but I'm still confused 🥲). Thanks again.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Heeeeello.

Gram: INT > Parts that boost INT > Heroes that prio INT > Easiest and main Falgship to get completed.

Oppo: ATK > Parts that boost ATK > Ion Heroes> 2nd easiest Flagship, not prio tho.

Demerzel: Trash > Leftovers when all othe Parts of other ships are 3/6/9 > Leftover Heroes > A joke.

Gram main with Kintetik, boost first Parts that improve Int, followed by all other parts. Get then to level 3 progression, then move on to Oppo. Oppo gets ATK, so fokus on ATK there. When all other parts are done, improve Demerzel parts by whatever your thumb is closest to (survival if you need to be optimal), and pull it also to 3.
Repeat for 6 and 9.

Heroes and Types remain the same.

Author: terrxy_42565
Content:
thank you very much for that! would you mind to explain what you mean with "3/6/9"? 

i am pretty much at the beginning of the game and was wondering about components and how much/how far i should upgrade them? i have like 2 epic (purple) components which i have leveled up to level 15, to other lower rarity components i leave at around level 10.

what would be the ideal progression to do for a low-spender?

**THANK YOU ALL SO MUCH FOR YOUR HARD WORK!!!** They should hire you!

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
To the windoooow to the waaaall. :D
(What 369 reminded me of when I saw it now)

Very welcome, glad to help.

Upgrade right how you feel like, you can use the parts you upgrade as material for the legendary parts you will obtain later, so... uhm... like prio Gram a little more then the others, but you will see that it gets harder and harder, so keep a... sort of balance.

3/6/9 refers to the progression with Prismatic Cores, that you will see later in game more. Those provide a bonus to your power if all parts have been upgraded to 3 or 6 or 9, all parts a single ship uses that is. Since costs increase alot, its best to get first Gram 3, then Oppo 3, then Dem 3 and then Gram to 6 and so on.

Author: tiddles6755
Content: Thanks very much for this guide! I have a question about Lily fragments through GvG. I've been playing around 2 months and I'm in the top guild for the server. While the guild provides many gift / rss benefits, i can never get into the top 10 for gvg (you're not kidding about it costing your soul). Should I look for a weaker guild, or is missing 5 fragments for Lily not a big deal (as a small spender I doubt i'll ever make it to top 3). I always buy the 20 Lily fragments in the valor shop.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Hi, very welcome.

I would not recommend to look for a smaller guild for that. Lily is a marathon for real, a smaller guild does  ot solve the issue with her.

You see its all rather complex and simple at the same time:
You have to be, essentially, better then all the players that do not care and just spend millions of points each day too much bc they can, and then be lucky to be in a match up that provides ample opportunity to score on PvP day.

Most of the time, for me that is, this system results in the following: we get matched up agains some guild that is far too weak. Or not there. Or just wants 1v1s. So in the end you stay up until 2 am for them to not show up at that time, then you have a regular human saturday, show up for 1 Fortress (where they dont show up), and you end up logging in in the eveing to see 3 of your guildmates (or the opponents whales) to have 100 million points. So you end up 20th even tho you made chest 9 every day.

So in the end: Just dont worry. Its a silly little hero that is designed to be stronger then the epic heros even at full green progression. Dont sweat it, dont be jealous. If you get the chance, try to pump it, but dont set up alarm clocks. Not worth the trouble.

Content:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ━━━━━━ ⚔️ Chapter XII - ADDENDUM² — WEAPONS, GUILD VOUCHERS & MORE ━━━━━━
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Overview:

This section exists for everything that didn’t quite fit anywhere else…
which, as it turns out, is quite a lot.

🔸 Covers newer systems and mechanics added over time
🔸 Focuses on features that impact progression but don’t belong in core sections
🔸 Will be expanded as the game continues to… evolve

➡️ Translation:
Whenever the game introduces something important but inconvenient to categorize,
it ends up here.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🔥 🔥 🔥 🔥 Weapons🔥 🔥 🔥 🔥

Quick Brief:

🔸 Type: Champion Equipment (because clearly we needed another system to optimize)
🔸 Variants: 11 total (2 Legendary, 9 Epic)
🔸 Core: Passive stat boosts + conditional abilities
🔸 Requirement: Match Weapon Type (Ion/Beam/Kinetic) AND Role (Attack/Defense/Support)
➡️ Translation: Use it correctly = massive power. Use it wrong = decorative stat stick.


Detailed:

Weapons are deceptively simple:
They always give stats, but only reward intelligence.

🧩 Passive Bonuses (Always Active):
🔸 Space Combat:
DMG Boost
Formation ATK / INT / DEF
🔸 Ground Combat:
Champion HP / ATK / INT / DEF

⚙️ Ability System (The real reason this exists):
Each weapon has:
1 Space Ability
1 Ground Ability
BUT:
❌ Wrong type/role → abilities disabled
✅ Correct match → full power unlocked

🔗 Matching Rules:
You must align:
⚡ Weapon Type (Ion / Beam / Kinetic)
🛡️ Champion Role (Attack / Defense / Support / Healing)

Weapon Types & Value:
🔶 Legendary Weapons:
🔸 Stronger versions of epic weapons
🔸 Higher stat bonuses
🔸 Enhanced abilities (especially on their intended champions)
🔶 Epic Weapons:
🔸 Same mechanics, lower numbers
🔸 Still provide meaningful buffs/debuffs


Scaling, Acquisition & Priority (aka: the important part)

Weapons scale through:
🔸 Weapon Energy → Levels
🔸 Fragments → Star upgrades
📊 Stat Value:
Example (Legendary, mid investment):
+1.8% DMG
+9.6% ATK
+7.2% INT
+4.8% DEF
➡️ Added directly to your % pools (% points)

🎲 Acquisition:
🔸 Weapon Prisms (Eternal Forge)
1 free daily
🔸 Additional Sources:
Guild Shop
Valour Shop
Intel Exchange
⚠️ Critical Priority Note:
Weapon materials are:
💀 Rare
💀 Expensive
💀 Extremely impactful

✅ ALWAYS prioritize:
Weapon Prisms
Weapon Fragments
Weapon Energy
➡️ Even when they feel overpriced

➡️ Best play:
Weapons are core progression, not side content
Weapons are not an addition.
They are the game quietly redefining what “strong” means.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🏊 🏊 🏊 🏊 Guild Vouchers² 🏊 🏊 🏊 🏊

Quick Brief:
🔸 Purpose: Currency for Guild Shop (core progression items)
🔸 Problem: You can’t buy everything weekly anymore
🔸 Solution: Prioritize consistently
➡️ Translation:
This is less “shopping” and more quiet long-term planning.


Income Sources (Weekly Reality Check):
Here’s what you can realistically expect:
🔸 Shrines
➡️ ~25k/day → ~175k/week
🔸 Sacred Tribute Ships
➡️ Up to ~20k/day → ~140k/week
⚠️ Requires coordination (10 joins/day, not guaranteed)
🔸 Guild Technology Assists
➡️ ~6k/day average → ~40–50k/week
🔸 Arms Race
➡️ ~226k every 3 days → ~450k/week
🔸 Defend the Trade Route
➡️ ~80k every 5 days → ~110k/week
🔸 Shadowfront
➡️ ~35k/week
🔸 GvG (SvS)
➡️ ~11.5k/day during event → ~70k/week avg
➡️ Chest rewards → ~40–50k/week
🔸 Operation Blackout (biweekly avg)
➡️ ~30k/week

➡️ Estimated Total:
👉 ~1,000,000 – 1,100,000 per week (with decent activity)


Priority System (What actually matters):
Since you can’t buy everything, here’s a recommended order:

🥇 Priority List:
1️⃣ Weapon Prisms (15x) — 450k
➡️ One of the highest-impact systems right now

2️⃣ Deep Space Beacons (90x) — 594k
➡️ Core late-game progression

3️⃣ AP Potions (10x) — 400k
➡️ Event flexibility & point generation

4️⃣ Echo Modules (1000x) — 600k
➡️ Crew progression (slow but necessary)

5️⃣ Radiant Aurora Fragments (5x) — 166.5k
➡️ Long-term legendary weapon progress

6️⃣ Weapon Energy Cores (3500x) — 350k
➡️ Needed for scaling weapons

⚠️ What looks good, but isn’t:
❌ Legendary Champion Fragments (5x) — 100k
➡️ Overpriced for the value they provide
❌ Epic Champion Shards
➡️ Not worth it at any stage

🚫 What to Ignore:

❌ Daily Shop — always skip
➡️ Yes, even “just this once”
➡️ Yes, even when resources feel tight

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
💰💰💰💰 Coins 💰💰💰💰

What It Is:
The true hidden late-game currency.

Used for:
🔸 Ship upgrades
🔸 Fleet component upgrades
🔸 Prismatic Core enhancements
🔸 Research
🔸 More research
🔸 Somehow even more research

➡️ Translation: The galaxy runs on Coins and bad financial decisions.

Why Coins Became MASSIVELY More Important:
The introduction of Dominion Warzone heavily increased the amount of available Computational Components (CC).
✅ Getting CC is now much easier than before
❌ Actually using all that CC is the problem

Because everything now demands absurd amounts of Coins.

➡️ Result:
Many players end up with giant stockpiles of CC…
…while lacking the Coins to upgrade anything.

Where Coins Come From:
✅ Trading (best long-term source)
✅ Events
✅ Daily activities
✅ Operation Blackout

Tips & Tricks:
✅ Coins now retain extremely high value even into late-game
✅ Don’t blindly focus only on CC farming
✅ Sustainable Coin income is what actually enables T7 progression
❌ Don’t underestimate Coin shortages — they become brutal later on
➡️ If you hit a major Coin bottleneck, see the Operation Blackout guide section.
➡️ Translation: A mountain of CC without Coins is just decorative engineering supplies.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
🚀 🚀 🚀 🚀 Gungir 🚀 🚀 🚀 🚀

Quick Brief:
🔸 Type: Premium Flagship
🔸 Role: Energy generation monster
🔸 Strength: Extremely high Champion ability cycling
🔸 Weakness: Horrifically paywalled
➡️ Translation: Possibly the strongest ship in the game, trapped behind a financial war crime.

Detailed:
When Gungir was announced, many players expected:
🔸 Another fancy stat-stick
🔸 Another “good but not amazing” premium ship
🔸 Demerzel 2.0

Instead:
✅ Gungir turned out to be absurdly strong.

Why?
Because modern combat in Galactic Frontier revolves around one thing:
✅ Champion abilities.

The majority of meaningful combat damage no longer comes from normal attacks.
It comes from:
🔸 Champion skill activations
🔸 Rotation timing
🔸 Energy generation
🔸 Ability cycling

And Gungir specializes in exactly that.

Core Strength:
Unlike other flagships:
❌ Gungir’s main ability does not focus on damage
❌ It does not focus on healing

✅ It generates massive amounts of Energy instead.

➡️ Translation: It weaponizes tempo itself.

Advanced Energy Mechanics:
After further testing and observation, the Energy differences between major flagships become extremely revealing.

Energy Generation / Control per full Champion cycle:

🔸 Gram:
✅ Guaranteed ~720 Energy gain

🔸 Opportunity:
✅ Guaranteed ~360 Energy gain
✅ PLUS 3 separate 60% chances to gain an additional 180 Energy
➡️ Translation: Opportunity is basically gambling addiction converted into ship mechanics.

🔸 Demerzel:
✅ Reduces enemy Energy recovery by ~10%
❌ Does NOT significantly accelerate your own rotations

🔸 Gungir:
✅ Guaranteed ~720 Energy gain
✅ PLUS reduces enemy ship Energy by a flat 300

➡️ Translation: Gram accelerates you. Demerzel slows them. Gungir politely decided to do both.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
What makes this important:
At first glance, Gungir can appear dramatically faster than Gram.

But looking deeper:
✅ Gram and Gungir actually trigger at relatively similar pacing
❌ The real difference is that Gungir ALSO suppresses enemy Energy generation simultaneously

➡️ Result:
✅ Your own Champion rotations remain extremely fast
✅ Enemy Champion abilities become delayed
✅ Burst windows become heavily one-sided
✅ Crowd control chains become more oppressive over time

➡️ Translation: The best crowd control is making the enemy wait their turn forever.

In practice:
🔸 Gungir can cycle abilities dramatically faster than most ships
🔸 It can feel close to firing nearly twice as often as older flagships
🔸 Gram remains surprisingly competitive in rotation speed, but still lacks the enemy suppression layer

Stat Scaling:
Another reason Gungir is so dangerous:
✅ It gains stacking combat bonuses similarly to Gram
✅ But its scaling ceiling is actually HIGHER

Comparison:
🔸 Gram: ~15% combat stat scaling
🔸 Gungir: ~25% scaling potential

💡 Important:
Gungir ramps up after a short combat window — usually around 2 Champion rotations.
After that:
✅ The ship becomes terrifying.

Rally Ability:
Gungir also gains bonuses when small ships die during rallies.
In theory:
🔸 Raids lose power over time
🔸 Gungir gains power instead

Reality:
❌ The effect sounds far crazier than it actually is.

Why?
Because:
✅ Small ships usually don’t die fast enough
✅ The advertised “100%” scaling is extremely misleading in real combat

➡️ Realistically:
You’re usually looking at something closer to:
🔸 ~5–20% actual effective value in practical fights

➡️ Translation: The marketing department and reality had a disagreement.

Author: h_o_l_l_y
Content:
Now For The Controversial Part:
Yes.
Demerzel has:
🔸 Debuffs
🔸 Utility

People constantly call it a “tank.”

❌ No. It isn’t.

The problem is simple:
✅ Opportunity provides vastly stronger damage scaling
✅ Opportunity provides superior Energy generation
✅ Modern combat heavily rewards faster Champion rotations
✅ Gungir pushes this even further

➡️ Result:
Demerzel simply gets outscaled.

Not “slightly.”
Not “situationally.”
➡️ Translation: The galaxy’s most expensive wet sponge remains a wet sponge.

The REAL Problem:
Gungir is acquired the same way as Demerzel.
Except:
❌ It is roughly 50% MORE expensive.

And that creates a massive issue:
✅ At equal upgrade levels, Gungir is FAR stronger than Demerzel
❌ But Gram and Opportunity remain dominant for most players due to blueprint accessibility

➡️ Translation: The strongest ship in the game may also be the least realistically obtainable.

Flagship Part Distribution:
Since you can only use 3 Flagships simultaneously:
✅ All 3 damage types still matter

Recommended setups:

For Whales:
🔸 Kinetic → Gungir
🔸 Beam → Gram
🔸 Ion → Opportunity

For Non-Whales:
If your Gungir is only around the same upgrade level as Demerzel:
🔸 Beam → Gungir
🔸 Kinetic → Gram
🔸 Ion → Opportunity

➡️ Translation: The flagship shuffle continues.

Final Verdict:
If availability and pricing did not exist:
✅ Gungir would likely be the strongest flagship in Galactic Frontier.

But reality exists.
And reality contains:
🔸 Blueprint scarcity
🔸 Extreme pricing
🔸 Human sadness

So while Gungir is potentially incredible:
❌ Most players will never realistically reach the level where it fully surpasses established Opportunity or Gram builds.
